Objective: Validate negative-edge triggered GPIO interrupt behavior across GPIO[8..39] with group enable and per-pin status clear.
TestSteps: The test initializes the error counter to zero. If built for GPIO0, it enables the corresponding GIC interrupt line and enables the GPIO0 interrupt at the system register; similarly, if built for GPIO1, it enables the GIC line and system register for GPIO1. It writes all ones to the shared pad-control register to establish a known state. It configures each of the 32 GPIO pads from 8 to 39 by writing to their control registers to enable the negative-edge detection and the necessary control bits, performing a short wait after each write. For each GPIO index from 0 to 31, it computes a one-hot mask for the current pad and clears any pending raw status for that pad, then enables the corresponding per-group interrupt mask bit and waits briefly. It sets a shared pending flag to indicate it is waiting for the interrupt. It writes all ones to the pad-control register to set the pads high, waits, and then writes the bitwise complement of the one-hot mask so that the targeted pad transitions low, thereby generating a negative edge. It enters a loop that waits for the interrupt service routine to clear the pending flag, checking periodically with a timeout to avoid hanging. If the timeout expires before the flag is cleared, it logs a timeout error for the specific pad. After processing all pads, it signals completion with the accumulated error count.
ExpectedResults: For each pad, a single negative-edge transition causes the ISR to run and clear the software pending flag. The per-group interrupt status reflects the targeted pad bit during service and is cleared after the ISR writes the raw status clear register. No timeout errors occur.
PassCriteria: The test passes if the software pending flag is cleared in response to each induced negative edge and the per-group interrupt status is observed and then cleared for each pad without any timeouts or residual status.
